title: Software Engineers Face a New Reality in the Age of AI
subtitle: As artificial intelligence takes over coding tasks, professionals are rethinking their careers and learning new ways to stay relevant.

summary.en: Software engineers are adapting to a workplace where artificial intelligence now writes most computer code. Many professionals feel anxious about their job security as traditional coding skills become less important. Experts suggest that the focus is shifting toward problem-solving and managing AI tools. Some engineers are returning to fundamental skills to maintain their value in the industry. The long-term future of the profession remains uncertain as companies continue to integrate new technology.
summary.zh: 軟體工程師正努力適應一個由人工智慧撰寫大部分程式碼的工作環境。隨著傳統程式編寫技能的重要性下降，許多專業人士對工作保障感到焦慮。專家建議，工作重心正轉向問題解決與管理人工智慧工具。部分工程師正回歸基礎技能，以維持在產業中的價值。隨著企業持續整合新技術，該職業的長期未來仍充滿不確定性。

paragraphs.en: The world of software engineering is changing faster than many professionals expected. Following recent reports on how artificial intelligence is reshaping the tech industry, it is becoming clear that the daily work of a programmer is no longer what it used to be. For many, the job has shifted from writing original code to reviewing and fixing work created by AI tools. This transition has left many engineers feeling uncertain about their future and the value of their traditional skills.
paragraphs.zh: 軟體工程領域的變遷速度，遠超乎許多專業人士的預期。隨著近期關於人工智慧如何重塑科技產業的報導不斷出現，顯而易見的是，程式設計師的日常工作已不再是過去那樣了。對許多人而言，這份工作已從撰寫原始程式碼，轉變為審核與修復由人工智慧工具所產出的內容。這種轉變讓許多工程師對自己的未來，以及傳統技能的價值感到不安。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the rise of AI has had a major impact on how software is built. For example, Google now reports that 75% of its code is written by artificial intelligence. This shift has created a difficult environment for workers who once viewed software engineering as a secure and high-paying career. Many engineers are now worried because their daily tasks have moved away from creative problem-solving and toward the management of automated systems. Some workers, like a software engineer named Matt, have started practicing their coding skills in their free time to ensure they do not lose their ability to write code without help.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，人工智慧的興起對軟體開發方式產生了重大影響。舉例來說，Google 現已指出，其百分之七十五的程式碼是由人工智慧所撰寫。這種轉變為那些曾將軟體工程視為穩定且高薪職業的從業人員，創造了艱困的環境。許多工程師現在感到擔憂，因為他們的日常任務已從創造性的問題解決，轉向管理自動化系統。一些從業人員，例如一位名叫麥特（Matt）的軟體工程師，已開始在閒暇時間練習程式編寫技能，以確保自己不會喪失獨立撰寫程式碼的能力。

paragraphs.en: This change comes at a time when the tech job market is already struggling. Data from Layoff.fyi shows that more than 600,000 tech workers in the United States have lost their jobs since the release of ChatGPT in 2022. Furthermore, reports from the New York Fed indicate that the unemployment rate for recent computer science graduates has risen to 7%. These figures highlight a difficult reality: while the demand for software has not disappeared, the way companies hire and use human talent is changing significantly.
paragraphs.zh: 這項變革發生的同時，科技業的就業市場也正陷入困境。根據追蹤裁員情況的網站 Layoff.fyi 數據顯示，自從二零二二年 ChatGPT 問世以來，美國已有超過六十萬名科技業員工失去工作。此外，紐約聯邦準備銀行（New York Fed）的報告指出，近期電腦科學畢業生的失業率已上升至百分之七。這些數據凸顯了一個嚴峻的現實：儘管對軟體的需求並未消失，但企業聘僱與運用人力資源的方式正在發生顯著改變。

paragraphs.en: Experts have different views on what this means for the future of the profession. Bouke Klein Teeselink, an assistant professor of economics at King’s College London, told The Guardian that the era of simply writing code is likely over. Instead, success now depends on how well an engineer can use AI to complete tasks. This view is supported by Ethan Mollick, a professor at the University of Pennsylvania’s Wharton School. He argues that the job is no longer about who can write the most lines of code. Instead, the focus must shift toward defining problems, designing complex systems, and directing AI tools to get the best results.
paragraphs.zh: 專家對於這對該職業的未來意味著什麼，有著不同的看法。倫敦國王學院（King’s College London）的經濟學助理教授布克·克萊恩·提瑟林克（Bouke Klein Teeselink）告訴《衛報》，單純撰寫程式碼的時代很可能已經結束。相反地，成功的關鍵現在取決於工程師運用人工智慧完成任務的能力。賓州大學華頓商學院（University of Pennsylvania’s Wharton School）教授伊森·莫利克（Ethan Mollick）也支持此觀點。他主張，這份工作不再是看誰能寫出最多的程式碼行數，重點必須轉向定義問題、設計複雜系統，以及指揮人工智慧工具以獲得最佳成果。

paragraphs.en: Despite these challenges, many engineers are not simply giving up. According to The Guardian, professionals are taking several steps to adapt to this new environment. Some are doubling down on the basics of computer science to ensure they understand the foundation of their work. Others are seeking new skills that AI cannot easily replace, such as high-level system architecture or project management. There is also a growing interest in collective action, where workers join together to ask for better protections and clearer expectations from their employers.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些挑戰，許多工程師並未輕言放棄。根據《衛報》報導，專業人士正採取多項措施以適應這個新環境。有些人加強鑽研電腦科學的基本功，以確保自己理解工作的根基；另一些人則尋求人工智慧難以輕易取代的新技能，例如高階系統架構或專案管理。此外，集體行動的呼聲也日益高漲，員工們團結起來，要求雇主提供更好的保障與更明確的職涯期望。

paragraphs.en: For many, the transition is deeply personal. Software engineering was once seen as a path to stability and high pay, with many practitioners earning double the national average salary. Now, the threat of layoffs and the pressure to use AI have made the future feel less certain. While companies continue to push for more AI integration, engineers are left trying to find their place in a system that is rapidly changing. The ability to evaluate AI-written code is becoming the most important skill for those who want to remain in the industry.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多人來說，這種轉變是極其個人的。軟體工程曾被視為通往穩定與高薪的途徑，許多從業者的薪資是全國平均水準的兩倍。如今，裁員的威脅與使用人工智慧的壓力，讓未來顯得更加不確定。當企業持續推動人工智慧的整合時，工程師們只能試圖在一個快速變動的系統中尋找自己的定位。對於那些希望留在產業內的人來說，評估人工智慧所寫程式碼的能力，正成為最重要的技能。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the role of the software engineer will likely continue to evolve. While some fear that their profession is disappearing, others see it as an opportunity to focus on more complex and meaningful work. The challenge for the next few years will be for workers to balance the efficiency of AI with the human expertise required to build reliable software. Whether this leads to a smaller, more specialized workforce or a new way of working remains to be seen. For now, the focus remains on learning, adapting, and finding ways to stay useful in a world where machines are increasingly doing the writing.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，軟體工程師的角色很可能會持續演變。雖然有些人擔心這個職業正在消失，但也有人將其視為專注於更複雜且具意義工作的機會。未來幾年的挑戰，在於員工如何平衡人工智慧的效率與打造可靠軟體所需的人類專業知識。這是否會導致人力需求縮減、專業化程度提高，或是形成一種全新的工作模式，仍有待觀察。目前，重點依然在於學習、適應，並在機器日益取代撰寫工作的世界中，設法保持自身價值。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To strengthen one's commitment to a particular strategy or course of action, especially in a risky situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 加倍投入、堅定立場（通常指在風險中仍堅持某種策略）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語源自賭博，意指加倍下注，這裡指更專注於基礎技能。文中：Some are doubling down on the basics of computer science to ensure they understand the foundation of their work.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Used to say that it is not yet certain or known what will happen in the future.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的慣用語，用來表達對未來的結果還無法定論。文中：Whether this leads to a smaller, more specialized workforce or a new way of working remains to be seen.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To move or change from one position, direction, or focus to another.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（輪班），這裡作動詞，描述工作性質的改變。文中：Instead, the focus must shift toward defining problems, designing complex systems, and directing AI tools to get the best results.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI

previousArticles.title: The Changing World of Software Engineering in the Age of AI
previousArticles.summaryEn: Software engineers are facing major changes as artificial intelligence begins to write most computer code. Many professionals feel anxious about their job security and the future of their careers. While coding skills were once the most important part of the job, the ability to manage AI tools is now becoming essential. Industry experts suggest that the role of an engineer is moving toward system design and problem solving. Workers are now looking for new ways to adapt, including learning new skills and seeking better protections.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is one specific action some engineers are taking to cope with the changing nature of their work?

options.A: They are quitting their jobs to pursue careers in project management.
options.B: They are practicing coding on their own time to maintain their independent skills.
options.C: They are collectively demanding that companies stop using AI tools.
options.D: They are focusing exclusively on learning how to write new lines of code.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段明確提到，像 Matt 這樣的工程師開始在空閒時間練習程式設計，以確保他們不會失去獨立寫程式的能力。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到的是尋求新技能，而非辭職；選項 C 錯誤，因為工程師是要求更好的保障與預期，而非完全禁止 AI；選項 D 錯誤，因為這與文章強調的「從寫程式轉向管理 AI」趨勢相反。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future job market for entry-level software engineers?

options.A: It will likely become easier to find jobs as AI makes the work faster.
options.B: It will become unnecessary as AI will eventually replace all human programmers.
options.C: It will likely require a shift toward higher-level system design rather than just coding.
options.D: It will prioritize candidates who have the most experience with traditional coding methods.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段與第六段的推論，專家如 Ethan Mollick 指出工作重心已轉向定義問題與設計系統，且第六段提到評估 AI 產出程式碼的能力至關重要。這意味著初階工程師若想生存，必須轉向更高層次的系統架構設計。選項 A 與文章中提及的失業率上升趨勢矛盾；選項 B 過於極端，文章並未斷言人類將完全被取代；選項 D 則忽視了文章強調的 AI 整合趨勢。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'doubling down' mean in the context of engineers adapting to the new environment?

options.A: Reducing their focus on basic tasks to save time.
options.B: Increasing their commitment to or investment in a particular strategy.
options.C: Working twice as hard to produce twice as much code as before.
options.D: Ignoring the basics to focus solely on complex AI management.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中 'doubling down on the basics' 指的是工程師加強對電腦科學基礎的投入與重視。這是一個慣用語，意指在面對挑戰時，反而更堅定地投入某項策略。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 將其誤解為單純的「產量翻倍」，而非「投入程度增加」；選項 D 則與原文強調「理解基礎」的意圖不符。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the impact of AI on software engineering?

options.A: AI has completely destroyed the software engineering profession.
options.B: Software engineers must stop using AI to protect their job security.
options.C: The role of the software engineer is evolving, requiring a shift from pure coding to managing AI and complex systems.
options.D: The tech industry is currently in a state of chaos with no clear path forward for employees.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章從不同角度探討 AI 如何改變軟體工程師的日常工作，並強調從「寫程式」轉向「定義問題、管理系統與評估 AI」的必要性。選項 A 過於悲觀，文章並未說職業已消失；選項 B 與專家建議相左；選項 D 雖然提到混亂，但文章最後一段明確指出了學習與適應的未來方向，並非完全沒有出路。
